steps: Set the two expressions for \(y\) equal: \(x - c = -6(x-12)^2\).
Expand and rearrange: \(-6(x^2 - 24x + 144) - x + c = 0 \Rightarrow 6x^2 - 143x + (864 - c) = 0\).
For two distinct real solutions the discriminant must be strictly positive: \(143^2 - 4 \cdot 6 \cdot (864 - c) > 0 \Rightarrow 20449 - 20736 + 24c > 0 \Rightarrow c > \frac{287}{24} \approx 11.96\).
Only \(c = 24\) satisfies \(c > 287/24\); the boundary value \(287/24\) gives exactly one solution (tangent), and 7 and 11 are both below the threshold.
choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — '7': \(7 < 287/24 \approx 11.96\), so discriminant \(< 0\); no real solutions. ✗
  • Choice B — '11': \(11 < 11.96\), so discriminant is still negative; no real solutions. ✗
  • Choice C — '\(\frac{287}{24}\)': Exactly at the threshold, discriminant \(= 0\); only one (repeated) solution, not two distinct ones. ✗
  • Choice D — '24': \(24 > 11.96\), so discriminant \(> 0\); two distinct real solutions. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
